title: Advancing Food Safety with Real-Time PCR for Rapid Pathogen Surveillance
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Foodborne     pathogens   pose   a     persistent     risk     to     public     health     and     food     security     worldwide  . Rapid   and     accurate     detection     methods   are   essential     to     prevent     outbreaks     and     ensure     consumer     protection  . Real-  Time     Polymerase     Chain     Reaction   (  qPCR  ) has   emerged   as   a     gold     standard     for     pathogen     surveillance     due     to     its     high     sensitivity  ,   specificity  ,   and     speed  .   Unlike     conventional     culture-based     methods  ,   qPCR     can     detect     viable     but   non-  culturable     microorganisms     within     hours  ,   reducing     response     time     during     foodborne     outbreaks  . The   integration     of     qPCR     into     food   testing   laboratories     and     industry     monitoring     programs     enhances     early     warning     systems     and     strengthens     overall     food     safety     frameworks  .   This     policy     brief     highlights     the     importance     of     scaling     up     qPCR-based     surveillance     to     safeguard     public     health     and     protect     food     supply     chains     from     microbial     threats  .
